DISCOVERY 302, OTHER GMP MANAGEMENT, INC., GMP CARS, LLC, GMP CARS COLLISION FAIRFIELD, LLC, GMP CARS COLLISION FREMONT, LLC, GMP CARS COLLISION SAN FRANCISCO, LLC, GMP CARS COLLISION SAN MATEO, LLC, GMP CARS COLLISION SAN RAFAEL, LLC, GMP CARS COLLISION SANTA CLARA, LLC, GMP CARS COLLISION SANTA ROSA, LLC, GMP CARS COLLISION VALLEJO, LLC'S MOTION TO QUASH OR MODIFY SUBPOENA SEEKING PRODUCTION OF PRIVATE FINANCIAL RECORDS AND FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER. THIRD-PARTY GMP ENTITIES MOTION TO QUASH IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS MADE THE NECESSARY SHOWING ITS NEED FOR PRIVATE BANK RECORDS OVERRIDES GMP ENTITIES' RIGHT OF PRIVACY. THEY ARE DIRECTLY RELEVANT TO THE ISSUES IN THE ACTION AND NOT READILY AVAILABLE FROM ALTERNATIVE OR LESS INTRUSIVE MEANS. (BRITT V. SUPERIOR COURT (1978) 20 CAL. 3D 844; ALLEN V. SUPERIOR COURT (1984) 151 CAL. APP. 3D 447. ) THE THIRD-PARTY'S RIGHT OF PRIVACY SHALL BE PROTECTED THROUGH A PROTECTIVE ORDER WHICH SETS SPECIFICALLY SETS FORTH THE PERSONS WHO ARE PERMITTED TO VIEW THE DOCUMENTS. THE PARTIES SHALL MEET AND CONFER ON THE TERMS OF THE PROTECTIVE ORDER. THE ORDER DENYING THE MOTION TO QUASH AND PROPOSED PROTECTIVE ORDER SHALL BE PREPARED BY THE PREVAILING PARTY AND SENT TO SBS@SBSLAWSF.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN STEIN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Records
Off Calendar Sep-29-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. NAZO S. SEMERDJIAN, Esq. of The Bernheim Law Firm (Tel: 818-760-7341) for Plaintiff Justice Investors, LP;
Atty. JASMINA E. ARAGON, Esq. of Cruser, Mitchell, Novitz, Sanchez, Gaston & Zimet LLP (Tel: 213-689-8500) for Defendant Adan M. Roldan.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ADAN ROLDAN'S Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Records.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the judge pro tem. The judge pro tem and counsel for both sides authorize the clerk to sign the stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Adan Roldan's motion to quash the subpoena for Wells Fargo bank records is denied. At the previous hearing the court ruled the bank records were discoverable but required further briefing on the issue of whether their production was violative of defendant's Fifth Amendment privilege. Further briefing was submitted and reviewed. The Fifth Amendment privilege does not apply to bank records. The Fifth Amendment protects a person asserting the privilege from compelled self-incrimination. In this situation no compulsion is present. A subpoena demanding production of bank documents does not compel oral testimony or compel the defendant to restate, repeat, or affirm the truth of the contents of the documents sought. United States v. Doe (1984) 465 U.S. 605; Rodriguez v. Fed. Sav. & Loan Ins. Corp. (N.D. Cal. 1989) 712 F. Supp. 159; Craib v. Bulmash (1989) 49 Cal.3d 475; People vs. Sanchez (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1012. A copy of this Order with Notice of Entry shall be served on Wells Fargo Bank who shall forward the subpoenaed bank records within twenty days of service. All documents produced shall be subject to the parties' stipulated Protective Order entered on August 18, 2020.

Judge Pro Tem: Steve Stein; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Off Calendar Feb-26-2020 Set For Trial On Feb-01-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Or Modify Subpoena Seeking Production Of Private Financial Records And For Protective Order
Off Calendar Sep-15-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Steven J. "Bernie" Bernheim Esq. (818) 760-7341 berniebernheim@gmail.com THE BERNHEIM LAW FIRM for Plaintiff.
Jasmina Aragon, Esq. 213-689-8501 of CRUSER, MITCHELL, NOVITZ, SANCHEZ, GASTON & ZIMET LLP for Defendant Adan Roldan.
David Bate, Esq. (310)348-2907 dhbate@gmail.com of BATE & BATE LLP for Other/Non Party Witnesses GMP et al.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

OTHER GMP MANAGEMENT, INC., GMP CARS, LLC, GMP CARS COLLISION FAIRFIELD, LLC, GMP CARS COLLISION FREMONT, LLC, GMP CARS COLLISION SAN FRANCISCO, LLC, GMP CARS COLLISION SAN MATEO, LLC, GMP CARS COLLISION SAN RAFAEL, LLC, GMP CARS COLLISION SANTA CLARA, LLC, GMP CARS COLLISION SANTA ROSA, LLC, GMP CARS COLLISION VALLEJO, LLC's Motion To Quash Or Modify Subpoena Seeking Production Of Private Financial Records And For Protective Order.

Third-party GMP entities motion to quash is denied. Plaintiff has made the necessary showing its need for private bank records overrides GMP Entities' right of privacy. They are directly relevant to the issues in the action and not readily available from alternative or less intrusive means. (Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 20 Cal. 3d 844; Allen v. Superior Court (1984) 151 Cal. App. 3d 447. ) The third-party's right of privacy shall be protected through a Protective Order which sets specifically sets forth the persons who are permitted to view the documents. The parties shall meet and confer on the terms of the Protective Order. The Order denying the motion to quash and proposed Protective Order shall be prepared by the prevailing party and sent to sbs@sbslawsf.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
